findings: The Swahili API bridge already explains API, endpoint, token, JSON, webhooks, SDK, pricing, auth, limits and production expectations.
The full API documentation remains English-only at /api/docs/ and should remain the source of truth for authentication, rate limits, request schemas, error codes and endpoint status.
Pricing is an English .html-backed route at /api/pricing, not /api/pricing/index.html; Swahili bridges should link to /api/pricing and label it as English-only pricing.

recommendedContent.item: Upgrade /sw/api/ as the Swahili API overview bridge
recommendedContent.scope: Add a clear Swahili overview of what the API is, what stays English-only, when to use docs/pricing, and which local developer tools are safe browser utilities.

recommendedContent.recommendedFile: defer or add section inside sw/api/index.html
recommendedContent.scope: Explain free key, paid pilot, rate limits and billing concepts in Swahili, while sending final pricing verification to /api/pricing.
validation: avoid copying live plan terms unless sourced from api/pricing.html

recommendedContent.recommendedFile: defer or add section inside sw/api/index.html
recommendedContent.scope: Define API key, token, endpoint, header, request, response, JSON, webhook, SDK and rate limit in Swahili context.
validation: do not expose or invent secrets

recommendedContent.scope: Use a static annotated request only after confirming the API docs endpoint examples remain valid.
validation: avoid claiming endpoint uptime or production readiness

recommendedContent.scope: Keep full docs English-only until an API-doc source-layer workflow exists.

recommendedPrompt133Action.reason: The safest useful conversion is to strengthen the existing Swahili API overview bridge instead of creating a second API page.
